ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater (Mango Sandstorm) spearphishing-to-RMM-installer chain (Atera/Syncro/ScreenConnect)
ucs.description: Detects MuddyWater's signature initial-access pattern: a recipient clicks an inbound link delivering a legitimate-but-abused Remote Management & Monitoring (RMM) installer (Atera, Syncro, ConnectWise ScreenConnect, RemoteUtilities, SimpleHelp), followed within 30 minutes by execution of that RMM agent on the recipient's host. This is the persistent, repeated tradecraft tracked by Microsoft (Mango Sandstorm), CrowdStrike (Static Kitten), Mandiant (TEMP.Zagros) and Proofpoint (TA450) against telecom, government and oil/gas victims in the Middle East, EU and US.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("AteraAgent.exe","atera_setup.msi","Syncro.msi","SyncroInstaller.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientSetup.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","RemoteUtilities.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","SimpleHelp.exe","AnyDesk.exe","N-Central.msi","TakeControl.exe","FixMe.exe","getscreen.exe") OR (Processes.process_name="msiexec.exe" AND Processes.process IN ("*atera.com*","*syncromsp.com*","*screenconnect.com*","*remoteutilities.com*","*simple-help.com*","*anydesk.com*","*getscreen.me*")) by _time, host, Processes.user, Processes.process_name, Processes.parent_process_name, Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` | join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as ClickTime from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.url IN ("*atera.com/getagent*","*syncromsp.com/dl*","*screenconnect.com*","*remoteutilities.com*","*simple-help.com*","*anydesk.com/download*","*getscreen.me*","*rport.io*") by host, Web.user, Web.url | `drop_dm_object_name("Web")` ] | where _time >= ClickTime AND _time <= (ClickTime+1800) | eval delaySec=_time-ClickTime | table _time, host, user, parent, process_name, cmdline, url, ClickTime, delaySec
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let CorrelationWindowMin = 30;
let RmmBinaries = dynamic(["AteraAgent.exe","atera_setup.msi","Syncro.msi","SyncroInstaller.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientSetup.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","RemoteUtilities.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","SimpleHelp.exe","AnyDesk.exe","FixMe.exe","getscreen.exe","TakeControl.exe"]);
let RmmDomains = dynamic(["atera.com","syncromsp.com","screenconnect.com","remoteutilities.com","simple-help.com","anydesk.com","getscreen.me","rport.io","fixme.it","n-able.com"]);
let SuspectClicks = EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EmailDirection == "Inbound" and DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
    | join kind=inner (
        EmailUrlInfo | project NetworkMessageId, Url, UrlDomain
      ) on NetworkMessageId
    | join kind=inner (
        UrlClickEvents
        | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
        | where ActionType in ("ClickAllowed","ClickedThrough")
        | project NetworkMessageId, ClickTime = Timestamp, AccountUpn, IPAddress
      ) on NetworkMessageId
    | project NetworkMessageId, ClickTime, AccountUpn, SenderFromAddress,
              SenderMailFromDomain, Subject, Url, UrlDomain;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ (RmmBinaries)
   or (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "msiexec.exe"
       and ProcessCommandLine has_any (RmmDomains))
   or (FolderPath has_any (@"\Users\", @"\Temp\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\")
       and FileName has_any (RmmBinaries))
| join kind=inner SuspectClicks on $left.AccountUpn == $right.AccountUpn
| where Timestamp between (ClickTime .. ClickTime + CorrelationWindowMin * 1m)
| project ClickTime, ProcessTime = Timestamp,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, ClickTime),
          DeviceName, AccountUpn, SenderFromAddress, SenderMailFromDomain,
          Subject, Url, UrlDomain,
          RmmBinary = FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| order by ClickTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let RmmBinaries = dynamic(["AteraAgent.exe","atera_setup.msi","Syncro.msi","SyncroInstaller.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientSetup.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","RemoteUtilities.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","SimpleHelp.exe","AnyDesk.exe","FixMe.exe","getscreen.exe"]);
let RmmDomainTokens = dynamic(["atera.com","syncromsp.com","screenconnect.com","remoteutilities.com","simple-help.com","anydesk.com","getscreen.me","rport.io","fixme.it"]);
let ProcSpawn = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend BinName = tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1])
    | where BinName in~ (RmmBinaries)
       or (BinName =~ "msiexec.exe" and CommandLine has_any (RmmDomainTokens))
    | project ProcTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, SubjectUserName, BinName,
              CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
let OfficeClick = OfficeActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where OfficeWorkload == "Exchange"
    | where Operation in~ ("MailItemsAccessed","Send","UrlClick")
    | extend NormalisedUser = tolower(UserId)
    | project ClickTime = TimeGenerated, NormalisedUser;
ProcSpawn
| extend NormalisedUser = tolower(SubjectUserName)
| join kind=leftouter OfficeClick on NormalisedUser
| where ClickTime between (ProcTime - 30m .. ProcTime)
| project ClickTime, ProcTime,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', ProcTime, ClickTime),
          Computer, Account, BinName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.rationale: MuddyWater pivoted away from custom POWERSTATS implants in 2022-2024 toward almost exclusive abuse of legitimate RMM agents — Atera and Syncro in particular are documented in Microsoft's Mango Sandstorm reporting, Proofpoint's TA450 'PDF lure' campaigns, and Deep Instinct/CYFIRMA write-ups against Israeli, Saudi, UAE and US targets. Few other state-aligned actors lean this hard on commodity RMM as

ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater PowGoop loader: goopdate.dll side-loaded outside Google Update
ucs.description: Hunts for MuddyWater's PowGoop downloader, which side-loads a malicious goopdate.dll into a renamed legitimate Google Update binary (GoogleUpdate.exe) staged outside %ProgramFiles%\Google. PowGoop is one of the few persistent custom tools the actor still uses (CISA AA22-055A, Mandiant, ClearSky) and the goopdate.dll filename is reused across nearly every MuddyWater intrusion 2021-2024 — including the Albanian government wiper deployment.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as path values(Filesystem.process_name) as writer values(Filesystem.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="goopdate.dll" by host, Filesystem.file_path, Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name("Filesystem")` | rex field=path "(?i)(?<dir>.*)\\\\goopdate\.dll" | where NOT match(dir, "(?i)\\\\Program Files.*\\\\Google") AND NOT match(dir, "(?i)\\\\Program Files \(x86\).*\\\\Google") | join type=outer host [ | tstats summariesonly=t values(Processes.process) as loadercmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("GoogleUpdate.exe","goopdate.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*goopdate*","*goon*.dll*","*Goon.dat*") by host, Processes.user, Processes.process_name ] | table _time, host, user, path, writer, process_name, loadercmd, parent
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let GoopParents = dynamic(["GoogleUpdate.exe","GoogleCrashHandler.exe","goopdate.exe"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName =~ "goopdate.dll"
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\")
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files\Google\")
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, ModulePath = FolderPath, ModuleSHA256 = SHA256,
          LoaderProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          LoaderPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          LoaderCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Account = InitiatingProcessAccountName
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where FileName =~ "goopdate.dll" or FileName =~ "Goon.dat" or FileName matches regex @"(?i)goon[a-z]*\.dll"
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, DroppedPath = FolderPath,
              Dropper = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
  ) on DeviceName
| extend SuspiciousLoader = iif(
    LoaderProcess !in~ (GoopParents) or
    LoaderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files", true, false)
| where SuspiciousLoader == true or isnotempty(DropperCmd)
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
ImRegistryEvent
| where 1 == 0 // unused branch — placeholder to keep ASIM imports valid
| union (
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where TargetFileName =~ "goopdate.dll"
        or TargetFileName matches regex @"(?i)^goon[a-z]*\.(dll|dat)$"
    | where not(TargetFilePath startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\")
    | where not(TargetFilePath startswith @"C:\Program Files\Google\")
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              TargetFilePath, TargetFileName, TargetFileSHA256,
              ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine
  )
| union (
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where TargetProcessName =~ "GoogleUpdate.exe"
        or TargetProcessCommandLine has "goopdate.dll"
        or TargetProcessCommandLine has "Goon.dat"
    | where not(TargetProcessName startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\")
    | where not(TargetProcessName startswith @"C:\Program Files\Google\")
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              TargetFilePath = TargetProcessName,
              TargetFileName = "GoogleUpdate.exe",
              TargetFileSHA256 = SHA256,
              ActingProcessName = ParentProcessName,
              ActingProcessCommandLine = TargetProcessCommandLine
  )
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: MuddyWater PowGoop goopdate.dll Side-Loaded Outside Google Update
id: 7e3a2b46-mwgp-1f0c-9e21-powgoop-side-load
status: experimental
description: Detects load or write of goopdate.dll from a path outside the legitimate Google Update install directories — a hallmark of MuddyWater's PowGoop loader (CISA AA22-055A, Mandiant, ClearSky).
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa22-055a
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/telegrams-of-compromise-static-kitten-targeting-uae
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.g0069
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ImageLoaded|endswith: '\goopdate.dll'
  filter_legit:
    ImageLoaded|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\'
      - 'C:\Program Files\Google\'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
fields:
  - Image
  - ImageLoaded
  - User
  - CommandLine
falsepositives:
  - Manual user-folder install of Chrome/Google Update (uncommon)
  - Software-deployment staging directories — baseline before alerting
level: high
ucs.rationale: PowGoop is one of MuddyWater's most distinctive sustained capabilities — described byname in the joint US/UK CISA AA22-055A advisory, Mandiant's TEMP.Zagros reporting, and ClearSky's POWERSTATS-to-PowGoop evolution writeup. The loader pattern is consistent across years of intrusions: a renamed legitimate signed GoogleUpdate.exe is dropped to a non-standard path (typically a user-writable directory
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
